journal_entry.description: Conversion of 50 preferred shares to 25 common shares
journal_entry.rationale: This is an equity conversion transaction. The carrying value of the preferred shares ($100 total, or $2 per preferred share) is removed from Preferred Shares (equity decrease, debit) and transferred to Common Shares (equity increase, credit) at the same carrying value. Under IFRS, conversion of shares at the stated carrying value is recorded at book value with no gain or loss. The debit tuple (e=1) correctly identifies one equity decrease line, and the credit tuple (b=1) correctly identifies one equity increase line. No tax applies to share conversions as they are capital structure changes, not taxable transactions.

output.reason: Converting preferred shares to common shares involves reducing (debiting) the Preferred Share Capital equity account and increasing (crediting) the Common Share Capital equity account. The debit to Preferred Share Capital = equity decrease. There is 1 debit line.

debit_classifier.reason: Converting preferred shares to common shares involves reducing (debiting) the Preferred Share Capital equity account and increasing (crediting) the Common Share Capital equity account. The debit to Preferred Share Capital = equity decrease. There is 1 debit line.

output.reason: Conversion of preferred shares to common shares is an equity-to-equity transaction. The credit side decreases preferred share capital (equity decrease, slot b=1) and simultaneously the debit side will increase common share capital. Since we're converting FROM preferred shares, the preferred share equity account is being reduced/debited, and common shares equity account is being increased/credited. For the CREDIT side: Common share capital increase = equity increase (1 line).

credit_classifier.reason: Conversion of preferred shares to common shares is an equity-to-equity transaction. The credit side decreases preferred share capital (equity decrease, slot b=1) and simultaneously the debit side will increase common share capital. Since we're converting FROM preferred shares, the preferred share equity account is being reduced/debited, and common shares equity account is being increased/credited. For the CREDIT side: Common share capital increase = equity increase (1 line).

output.reason: Entry correctly records conversion of preferred shares to common shares at carrying value. Debit to Preferred Shares (equity decrease, $100) and credit to Common Shares (equity increase, $100) are directionally correct. Entry balances (debits = credits = $100). Under IFRS, share conversions are recorded at book value with no gain/loss recognition. No tax applies to capital structure changes. All elements verified correct.
